summary: 8 concrete items surfaced from reachable HN front-page comments.

items.name: Rocksky
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Rocksky – Music scrobbling and discovery on the AT Protocol
items.summary: Music scrobbling and discovery on AT Protocol.
items.why_included: A real Show HN app with clear comment momentum and a strong recommendation angle.
items.evidence: Commenters called it a perfect recommendation model and said they would add support in another app.

items.name: Windows 9x Subsystem for Linux
items.summary: A Codeberg project that brings Windows 9x nostalgia to Linux.
items.why_included: The repo is concrete, clickable, and commenters immediately reacted to the UI polish.
items.evidence: Replies praised the clean, usable interface and said it looked like GitHub/Forgejo.

items.name: Accelerate
items.source_story_title: Accelerate – Embedded language for high-performance array computations
items.summary: A Haskell array language with JIT, CPU, and GPU offload.
items.why_included: A mature performance library with unusually clear utility in the comment thread.
items.evidence: Commenters described it as NumPy plus a JIT compiler and highlighted automatic CPU/GPU vectorization.

items.name: PART Telescopes
items.source_story_title: PART Telescopes – Bringing radio astronomy within reach of rural schools
items.summary: Low-cost radio telescope kits for rural schools.
items.why_included: A concrete physical project with direct educational value and strong comment links to similar efforts.
items.evidence: One commenter said low-cost radio telescopes are great fun and linked several related projects.

items.name: Greek Alphabet Cards
items.summary: Interactive cards for learning the Greek alphabet.
items.why_included: Small, concrete, and surprisingly useful for math-heavy work.
items.evidence: Commenters said the cards were very handy and that learning Greek letters paid off in university.

items.name: SANA-WM
items.source_story_title: SANA-WM, a 2.6B open-source world model for 1-minute 720p video
items.summary: An open-source world model for 1-minute 720p video.
items.why_included: A noteworthy model release with visible HN discussion about what world models can do.
items.evidence: Commenters said they were impressed with the world models and pointed to robotics simulation as an immediate use case.

items.name: Kioxia 10 PB 2RU server
items.source_story_title: Kioxia and Dell cram 10 PB into slim 2RU server
items.summary: A slim 2RU server packing 10 PB of storage.
items.why_included: A striking hardware build that commenters immediately zoomed in on for density and drive choice.
items.evidence: Replies called the Kioxia drives the interesting part and said the density was fascinating.

items.name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash
items.source_story_title: DeepSeek-V4-Flash means LLM steering is interesting again
items.summary: A blog post on LLM steering vectors for DeepSeek-V4-Flash.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced a concrete steering technique discussion around a current model.
items.evidence: A commenter said steering features could remove refusals, and others compared runtime steering with modified weights.
